name: Download from PixVerse
description: Downloads images from PixVerse account using the CLI. The user must have PixVerse open in their browser and be logged in. The CLI uses the browser session cookies for authentication.
prerequisites: User has PixVerse (pixverse.ai) open and logged in in their browser
The `pixverse` CLI is available via `npx pixverse`
The CLI is authenticated (shares cookies with the browser session)

steps.notes: Returns JSON with all favorited images. Each item has: asset_id, created_at (UTC timestamp), image_url, model, quality. Use --page 2, --page 3 etc. to paginate if more than 100 items.

steps.action: Filter by date and time range
steps.notes: Parse the created_at field (format: 2026-05-05T07:03:20Z) to filter images within the requested date and time window. The timestamp is in UTC. Convert to local time if needed (e.g., CEST = UTC+2). Collect all matching asset_ids.
steps.example_filter: Filter for May 5th between 07:00-08:20 UTC: keep items where created_at starts with '2026-05-05' and time portion is >= '07:00' and <= '08:20'

steps.notes: Downloads the image to the specified directory. The CLI auto-names files as pixverse_image_<id>_<timestamp>.png. Create the output directory first with mkdir -p.

Build an image list with objects: { id, url, note, selected: false, improve4k: false, splitGrid: false }. The url must be relative to public/, e.g., /assets/storyboard/uploads/task36-pixverse/filename.png. Create a Pass 1 object and update both the task JSON file (tasks/current/<task-id>.json) and storyboard-data.json.
Set task status to 'pass', set activePassId to 'pass-1', set generatedImages to the image list. The UI polls storyboard-data.json and will auto-refresh.
